summary: Iter 19 — Free public AI Audit (/audit), AB Tester (subject/headline/CTA), WhatsApp Broadcast publish channel. 16/16 backend pytest pass. Frontend: /audit public flow (form → result with score/issues/3 ideas/CTA → reset), Landing 'Free SEO audit' pill → /audit, ABTester embedded in /content kit detail (3 variants + WINNER badge generated successfully), Schedule modal now exposes 'WhatsApp blast' option with green WhatsApp logo. Admin login → /admin redirect still works. Existing platforms still selectable.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is now ~5719 lines — at minimum split out the AB-test + Free-audit blocks (~lines 2484-2722) into /app/backend/lead_magnets.py. The file has crossed every reasonable maintainability bar.

_scrape_url_quick uses synchronous `requests` inside `run_in_executor` — fine, but timeout=10s + chained AI call (~5s) makes /free-audit single-request latency 6-15s. Consider caching scrape result by URL hash for 24h to deflect repeated abuse from the same URL.

Free-audit rate limit is per-email only (5/hour). An attacker can rotate emails. Add a per-IP limit too (e.g. 10/hour per source IP) — public unauth endpoint with outbound HTTP fetch is a SSRF/DDoS surface.

_scrape_url_quick does NOT block private IP / localhost / metadata IP ranges. SSRF risk: a user could pass http://169.254.169.254/ or http://localhost:8001/api/admin/users to probe internal services. MUST add IP allowlist/denylist before fetch.
Free-audit endpoint accepts any `business_name` and stores it raw in audit_leads — add length cap (e.g. 200 chars) to avoid bloat.
AB test endpoint silently falls back to 'highest CTR uplift' if the AI returns an out-of-range recommended_index — this is fine but the fallback path is not unit-tested.
FreeAuditIn does NOT use Pydantic EmailStr — only checks for '@'. e.g. 'a@' passes initial validation. Use EmailStr for stricter validation.
_publish_to_whatsapp_broadcast picks the FIRST social_post body — for kits with multi-platform posts that's non-deterministic; consider preferring kit.whatsapp_post if present.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (all 6 user-flows verified: /audit public form, audit result with score 59/100 + 6 issues + 3 idea cards + convert CTA, New audit reset, landing-free-audit pill → /audit, ABTester 3 variants + WINNER + copy buttons + reasoning, Schedule modal WhatsApp blast tile)

seed_data_creation: Created 8+ ephemeral audit_runs entries (preview@zeromark.ai, persist-*, audit-test-*, rl-* emails) and 4+ audit_leads. Generated 5 ab_tests records under admin user (subject/headline/cta variants). Created 1 WhatsApp-broadcast schedule (PENDING) under admin.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All iter19 features green. Important data-testids: free-audit-page, audit-form, audit-url-input, audit-email-input, audit-biz-input, audit-submit, audit-result, audit-score, audit-idea-{0..2}, audit-convert-cta, landing-free-audit, ab-tester, ab-kind-{subject|headline|cta}, ab-base-text, ab-audience, ab-n, ab-run, ab-result, ab-variant-{0..n}. AB-tester is INSIDE a <details><summary>⚡ TRY A/B variants for this title</summary> on the blog-post panel — must click summary first. Schedule modal is reached by clicking the '+ Schedule' label in any empty calendar cell on /schedule (NOT a top-right 'New schedule' button). 'WhatsApp blast' tile uses green color. Backend rate-limit on /free-audit is 5/hour per email (returns 429). Free-audit unreachable URL returns 400 with 'Could not fetch site: ...'. _publish_to_whatsapp_broadcast gracefully returns mock_published when no Twilio env. SECURITY ISSUE FLAGGED: /free-audit is SSRF-vulnerable — escalate to main agent.
rca of the issue: No bugs found. Fixed one false-positive in my own test (schedule POST returns {'schedule': {...}} wrapper, not the doc directly).
